title: Hydraulics
point: Transmission and control of forces and movements by fluids

title: Pneumatics
point: System using air as working medium

title: Hydraulic System
point: System using oil or water as working medium

title: Hydraulic Medium
point: Oil is the most commonly used hydraulic fluid

title: Water Usage
point: Water is rarely used in present-day hydraulic applications

title: Velocity
point: Average speed of fluid particles past a point

title: Low Velocity
point: Low fluid velocity reduces friction losses

title: Laminar Flow
point: Fluid particles move parallel to flow path

title: Turbulent Flow
point: Fluid particles move haphazardly and non-parallel

title: Turbulent Flow
point: Turbulent flow wastes energy as heat

title: Flow Rate
point: Volume of liquid passing a point per unit time

title: Flow Rate Unit
point: Flow rate commonly measured in LPM

title: Flow Rate Unit
point: Flow rate commonly measured in GPM

title: Atmospheric Pressure
point: Atmospheric pressure at sea level is 14.7 psi

title: Atmospheric Pressure
point: Atmospheric pressure at sea level is 1.03 kg/cm²

title: Flow
point: Flow causes piston motion in a cylinder

title: Flow
point: Flow occurs due to pressure difference

title: Pump Function
point: Pump usually produces flow in hydraulic systems

title: Load Movement
point: Insufficient pressure cannot move the load

title: Hydraulic Oil
point: Hydraulic oil acts as a lubricant

title: Oil Pressure
point: One foot oil column produces about 0.4 psi

title: Water Pressure
point: 10 m water column produces about 1 kg/cm²

title: Flow Requirement
point: Pressure drop across restriction is necessary for flow

title: Flow Relation
point: No flow means no pressure drop

title: Cylinder Force
point: Cylinder force depends on pressure and piston area

title: Cylinder Speed
point: Cylinder speed depends on flow rate and piston area

title: Pipe Velocity
point: Fluid velocity varies inversely with square of pipe diameter

title: Friction
point: Friction in pipes causes pressure drop

title: Air vs Oil
point: Air is compressible while oil is practically incompressible

title: Suction Principle
point: Atmospheric pressure pushes oil into pump suction

title: Flow Path
point: Flow takes path of least resistance

title: Flow Direction
point: Flow moves from higher pressure to lower pressure

title: Flow Rate
point: Flow rate directly proportional to pressure difference

title: Pipe Diameter
point: Larger pipe diameter reduces pressure drop

title: Pascal Law
point: Pressure applied on confined fluid is transmitted undiminished

title: Pascal Law
point: Pressure acts equally in all directions

title: Pascal Law
point: Pressure acts at right angles to surfaces

title: Hydraulic Press
point: Application of Pascal's law for force multiplication

title: Hydraulic Press
point: Pressure remains uniform throughout confined fluid

title: Hydraulic Press
point: Work done remains same on both pistons

title: Bernoulli Principle
point: Total energy remains constant in flowing fluid

title: Bernoulli Principle
point: Sum of pressure kinetic and potential energy is constant

title: Hydraulic Advantage
point: Hydraulics transmit large forces over long distances

title: Power Density
point: Hydraulic systems have high power-to-weight ratio

title: Motor Comparison
point: Hydraulic motor weighs about one-seventh equivalent electric motor

title: Overload Protection
point: Relief valve provides built-in overload protection

title: Load Holding
point: Pilot-operated check valve holds suspended load

title: Design Flexibility
point: Hydraulic circuits can be modified easily

title: Maintenance
point: Contamination control is key maintenance task

title: Variable Speed
point: Hydraulics provide infinitely variable speed control

title: Stalling
point: Loads can be stalled without equipment damage

title: Cooling
point: Hydraulic fluid carries away heat automatically

title: Wear
point: Hydraulic components have low wear due to lubrication

title: Compressibility
point: Fluid volume reduces about 0.7% at 100 bar increase

title: Air Entrapment
point: Entrapped air causes noise vibration and jerky motion

title: System Design
point: Pressure and flow losses must be considered

title: Reservoir
point: Stores supplies returns and protects hydraulic oil

title: Baffle Plates
point: Baffle plates help cooling and settling of oil

title: Suction Valve
point: Suction valve must be opened before pump start

title: Suction Line
point: Hose compensates minor misalignment and vibration

title: Pump
point: Pump does not create pressure directly

title: Hydraulic Pumps
point: Only positive displacement pumps are used

title: Centrifugal Pump
point: Centrifugal pump is non-positive displacement type

title: Pump Types
point: Gear vane and piston pumps are common

title: Pump Operation
point: Never start positive displacement pump with closed suction

title: Aeration
point: Air entering pump causing noise and damage

title: Pump Accessories
point: Pump delivery followed by relief valve

title: Pump Accessories
point: Pump delivery followed by pressure gauge

title: Pump Accessories
point: Pump delivery followed by check valve

title: Check Valve
point: Allows flow in one direction only

title: Check Valve
point: Check valve prevents reverse pump rotation

title: Relief Valve
point: Opens when pressure exceeds set value

title: Accumulator Types
point: Bladder piston dead-weight and direct gas loaded

title: DC Valve
point: Can stop start or reverse actuator

title: DC Valve Actuation
point: Lever cam solenoid pedal pneumatic hydraulic

title: Solenoid Valve
point: Most commonly used DC valve actuation

title: Needle Valve
point: Can act as flow control valve

title: Sequence vs Relief
point: Sequence valve is similar but not same as relief valve

title: Pressure Reducing Valve
point: Output pressure cannot exceed set value

title: Reducing vs Relief Valve
point: Pressure reducing valve differs from relief valve

title: Filter Media
point: Paper wire mesh and synthetic fiber used

title: Strainer
point: Coarse suction filter is called strainer

title: Pressure Switch Types
point: Plunger type and Bourdon tube type

title: Float Switch
point: Operates due to buoyancy in oil

title: Hydraulic Problems
point: More than 80% due to contaminated fluid

title: Cooler Load
point: Heat load considered 25–40% of pump kW

title: Common Exchangers
point: Shell-and-tube and plate exchangers most common

title: Single Acting Cylinder
point: Can only push or lift load

title: Single Acting Cylinder
point: Retracts by weight spring or load

title: Seal
point: Prevents fluid movement in undesired direction

title: Seal Importance
point: Small cost but major effect on efficiency

title: Seal Handling
point: Do not use sharp tools on seals

title: Piping
point: Seamless carbon steel pipes commonly used

title: Pipe Joints
point: Union joints used for small pipes

title: Pipe Joints
point: Flange joints used for large pipes

title: Hose Layout
point: Hoses should not twist rub or entangle

title: Hydraulic Fluids
point: Mineral vegetable synthetic oil and water

title: Water Use
point: Pure water seldom used as hydraulic fluid

title: HFC Fluid
point: Offers highest degree of fire resistance

title: Fluid Property
point: Hydraulic fluid should have anti-foaming characteristics

title: Fluid Property
point: Hydraulic fluid should have high flash point

title: Flash Point
point: Temperature at which oil just ignites

title: Fire Point
point: Temperature at which oil burns continuously

title: Contamination Control
point: Oil contamination is number one enemy of hydraulics

title: Contamination Source
point: Wear of pumps valves and cylinders

title: Fresh Oil
point: Fresh drum oil may be class 10 or 11

title: Water Contamination
point: Water turns oil whitish and foamy

title: Leakage Control
point: Oil is lifeblood of hydraulic system

title: Hydraulic Symbol
point: Check valve represented by one-way flow symbol

title: Hydraulic Symbol
point: Single and double acting cylinders have different symbols

title: Pump Arrangement
point: Pump generally followed by relief valve

title: Pump Arrangement
point: Pump generally followed by check valve

title: Open Circuit
point: Oil returns to tank after use

title: Closed Circuit
point: Oil returns directly to pump suction

title: Closed Circuit
point: Booster pump adds cool oil through flushing valve

title: Steel Plant Applications
point: Rolling mills electric arc furnaces cranes coke ovens blast furnaces CCS presses machine tools

title: Cavitation
point: Pressure falls to liquid vapor pressure

title: Cavitation
point: Small suction pipe can cause cavitation

title: Compressibility
point: Volume change per unit pressure change

title: Decompression
point: Gradual pressure release of confined fluid

title: Hydraulic Hammering
point: Pressure surge due to sudden valve action

title: Cracking Pressure
point: Pressure at which relief valve starts opening

title: Pressure Override
point: Difference between cracking and full-flow pressure

title: Teflon Tape
point: Avoid Teflon tape in precision systems

title: Maintenance
point: Prevent cooling water entry into system

title: Maintenance
point: Store seals in cool dry place

title: Maintenance
point: Prefer unit replacement over field repair

title: Maintenance
point: Change all seals when opening components

title: Accumulator
point: Precharge pressure at least one-fourth working pressure

title: Pump Start
point: Fill pump with oil and open suction valve before startup
